purpose_string: Replace the mixed Blender-reference/browser-guide scene used by v10 with one pinned browser Three.js static scene for all six LTX guide frames while retaining the canonical actionless model and camera as immutable provenance.

predecessor_experiment_object.result_string: The four intended swing phases became visible, but mixed-renderer endpoint conditioning produced severe exposure/background drift, paired-swing overlap and a loop seam; the result remains rejected.

positive_prompt_string: Locked-camera inverse skeletal fitting reference of the exact canonical actionless horse and camera. All six supplied guides come from one browser Three.js static scene with identical background, exposure and framing. Preserve one identical horse, its geometry, proportions, materials, four persistent legs and four compact hooves. Frame 0 and frame 48 are the byte-identical browser static-scene endpoint guide. Follow the four browser swing-apex guides in this exact order: hind-left hoof alone at frame 6 and fully touching down by recovery frame 12; fore-left hoof alone at frame 18 and fully touching down by recovery frame 24; hind-right hoof alone at frame 30 and fully touching down by recovery frame 36; fore-right hoof alone at frame 42 and fully touching down by recovery frame 48. The next hoof must not begin lifting before the previous hoof has completed touchdown. At most one hoof is in swing at any frame; four-hoof all-stance recovery frames are allowed, and at least three hooves remain planted with no flight or suspension frame. Interpolate one smooth anatomically correct four-beat walk cycle in place through those poses. Preserve the supplied background and exposure without net translation, camera motion or seam pause.
negative_prompt_string: camera movement, crop, zoom, scene cut, background change, background fade, ground disappearance, exposure drift, lighting flicker, duplicate horse, identity change, extra limb, missing limb, paired hoof swing, overlapping hoof swing, diagonal pair, lateral pair, trot, pace, hop, bound, gallop, flight phase, suspension phase, fewer than three planted hooves, foot sliding, hoof skating, ground penetration, broken joint, changing segment length, rubber limb, curled distal limb, hoof morphing, material change, endpoint freeze, text, watermark
controlled_variable_string: The v10 seed, model provenance, canonical actionless camera, prompt family, resolution and guide strengths remain fixed; all six conditioning images now come from one browser static-scene renderer and the canonical source RGB is provenance-only.
fixed_factors_array: same immutable actionless Horse model and camera provenance
same pinned LTX 19B FP8 checkpoint and static-camera LoRA
same four single-hoof swing apex targets
no Blender and no unavailable control model
manual_acceptance_gates_array: frame 0 and frame 48 browser PNG SHA-256 values are byte-identical
browser endpoint SHA-256 explicitly differs from the canonical source reference PNG while retaining its model and camera provenance
all six guide PNGs are browser-rendered under one pinned static-scene settings contract
staticSceneQa status is PASS with zero background channel and mean-luma drift
all four browser swing-apex PNGs show exactly one lifted hoof in the required order
one persistent horse with four persistent compact hooves
hind-left, fore-left, hind-right and fore-right touchdown is complete by frames 12, 24, 36 and 48 respectively
the next hoof does not begin lifting until the previous hoof has completed touchdown
no paired or overlapping swing frame, no flight or suspension frame, and at least three planted supporting hooves at every frame
TAPNext gait QA orderAccepted and overlapAccepted are both true
candidate remains generated_not_approved until browser fitting and visual QA pass

generation_authorization_object.required_next_action_string: Runtime authorization must exactly match this immutable experiment id.
